studio-charcoal.$description: Studio Charcoal — Page canvas and card surfaces — the floor of the dark gallery, never pure black so the eye can rest on edge softness

bone-white.$description: Bone White — Primary text, hairline borders on images, and the rare inverted surface. Carries all type hierarchy on its own

ash-gray.$description: Ash Gray — Muted text, subtle card borders, inactive labels — the only middle gray in the scale, used when white is too loud

mid-felt.$description: Mid Felt — Light surface wash for inverted panels or modal scrims — the off-white that sits between bone and the dark canvas

carbon.$description: Carbon — Deepest accent for icon strokes and contrast borders where the charcoal canvas is too light to hold an edge

ember-orange.$description: Ember Orange — Filled primary action button — the only chromatic surface in the UI, always paired with a 30px orange halo so it glows like a filament; 30px outer glow on the primary CTA — duplicates the Ember Orange hex but exists as a box-shadow, not a fill, so the button appears lit from within

lavender-link.$description: Lavender Link — Inline link color and the outlined secondary action border — a desaturated cool counterpoint to the warm orange ember, keeping actions readable against charcoal

gt-flexa.$description: Display and heading workhorse. Weight 200 carries every headline above 24px — the 200 weight is the signature: at 68px and 86px the letterforms dissolve into atmosphere rather than shout. Weight 400 steps in for sub-headings and card captions where a whisper isn't enough. Line-heights collapse toward 1.0 at the largest sizes so display type stacks like a column of breath.

tobias-light.$description: Secondary display voice for section headers and the footer wordmark. Used at a step below GT-Flexa 200 to create tonal contrast — Tobias feels more architectural, more labeled, while GT-Flexa feels ambient. Negative letter-spacing tightens the wide proportions of the Tobias Light cut.

times.$description: Body text and hero paragraph copy — a deliberate serif counterpoint to the geometric display fonts. The serif adds warmth and editorial gravity to product descriptions without ever growing larger than body size.

studio-floor.$description: Surface level 0: Page background — the base of the gallery

card-matte.$description: Surface level 1: Feature cards and media frames sit on the same charcoal as the canvas, defined by 1px borders rather than surface shifts

inverted-panel.$description: Surface level 2: Light scrim or modal surface when the system inverts
